3529	  % (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L691--719).
3530	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $R$ be a ring. Let $M$ be an $R$-module. Let $\widetilde M$
3531	  % be the sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$-modules
3532	  % associated to $M$.
3533	  % \begin{enumerate}
3534	  % \item We have $\Gamma(\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}) = R$.
3535	  % \item We have $\Gamma(\Spec(R), \widetilde M) = M$ as an $R$-module.
3536	  % \item For every $f \in R$ we have
3537	  % $\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}) = R_f$.

3539	  % as an $R_f$-module.

3541	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Tag 01HV, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves}.}
3542	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
3543	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Writing \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global sections,
3544	  there is a natural isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
3545	  under which \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F}) = M_f\) for every \(f \in R\); in particular the sections of
3546	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over a distinguished open \(D(f)\) are the away localisation \(M_f\). This is the
3547	  affine quasi-coherent structure theorem (Tag 01HV, with the equivalence quasi-coherent-on-affine
3548	  \(\simeq\) \(R\)-modules, Tag 01I8).

3550	\begin{proof}
3551	  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}
3552	  \leanok
3553	  On an affine scheme the global-sections functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) and
3554	  the tilde functor \(M \mapsto \widetilde{M}\) are mutually inverse equivalences between
3555	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and \(R\)-modules; the counit
3556	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\) is an isomorphism for quasi-coherent
3557	  \(\mathcal{F}\). By Tag 01HV the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(f)\) are \(M_f\), so the
3558	  isomorphism identifies \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) with \(M_f\).

3560	  Concretely, the isomorphism is the inverse of the tilde–\(\Gamma\) counit: once the counit
3561	  \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma} : \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\) is known to
3562	  be an isomorphism, the asserted iso \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})}\) is its
3563	  inverse (the one-line conditional argument formalized). The hypothesis that the counit is an
3564	  isomorphism holds for any \(\mathcal{F}\) admitting a global presentation
3565	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}), and unconditionally for quasi-coherent
3566	  \(\mathcal{F}\) once the 01I8 instance \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow
3567	  \operatorname{IsIso}(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma})\) is supplied (see the decomposition remark
3568	  below).
3569	\end{proof}

3617	\begin{remark}[The 01I8 decomposition: quasi-coherence \(\Rightarrow\) counit is iso]
3618	  \label{rem:o1i8_decomposition}
3619	  \uses{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation,
3620	    lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation}
3621	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Tag 01I8, Lemma
3622	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof) (read from
3623	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1287--1387).
3624	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3625	  % Since every standard open $D(f)$ is quasi-compact we see that
3626	  % $X$ is locally quasi-compact ... Hence by Modules, Lemma
3627	  % \ref{modules-lemma-quasi-coherent-module} for every prime $\mathfrak p \subset R$
3628	  % corresponding to $x \in X$ there exists an open neighbourhood
3629	  % $x \in U \subset X$ such that $\mathcal{F}|_U$ is isomorphic to the quasi-coherent
3630	  % sheaf associated to some $\mathcal{O}_X(U)$-module $M$. ... Now Algebra, Lemma
3631	  % \ref{algebra-lemma-glue-modules} shows that there exist an $R$-module $M$ such that
3632	  % $M_i = M_{f_i}$ compatible with the morphisms $\psi_{ij}$. ... By construction this
3633	  % map reduces to the isomorphisms $\varphi_i^{-1}$ on each $D(f_i)$ and hence is an
3634	  % isomorphism."
3635	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Tag 01I8, \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
3636	  (proof).}
3637	  The unconditional quasi-coherent statement
3638	  \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow \mathcal{F} \cong
3639	  \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\) reduces, through
3640	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}, to the single instance
3641	  \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow
3642	  \operatorname{IsIso}(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma})\) on the affine base \(\operatorname{Spec} R\).
3643	  This is the content of Stacks Tag 01I8, organised here as a three-step route into the
3644	  presentation-form discharge:
3645	  \begin{enumerate}
3646	  \item \emph{Global generation.} A quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) is
3647	    generated by its global sections: one produces a global epimorphism
3648	    \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Quasi-coherence gives generators only
3649	    locally on a basic-open cover; these are globalised using the quasi-compactness of
3650	    \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) and the localisation-of-sections property of quasi-coherent sheaves
3651	    (sections over \(D(f)\) are the away localisation), the affine global-generation statement of
3652	    Tag 01I8.
3653	  \item \emph{Presentation.} The kernel of \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is
3654	    again quasi-coherent on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), hence again globally generated by step (1);
3655	    composing yields a global presentation \(\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{Presentation}\).
3656	  \item \emph{Counit iso.} Feeding that presentation to
3657	    Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation} makes
3658	    \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) an isomorphism; equivalently, this is the discharge of
3659	    Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}.
3660	  \end{enumerate}
3661	  Step (1) is the load-bearing affine global-generation input; steps (2)--(3) are formal. The same
3662	  route is recorded as the route to the affine equivalence \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec}
3663	  R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\) (Tag 01I8, \texttt{lemma-equivalence-quasi-coherent}).
3664	\end{remark}

3671	    def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}
3672	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG, Lemma
3673	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (proof, condition (3)) (read
3674	  % from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L172--173).
3675	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
3676	  % Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
3677	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG
3678	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, proof).}
3679	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
3680	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then \(\mathcal{F}\) has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for the
3681	  affine cover system (Definition~\ref{def:affine_cover_system},
3682	  Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}): for every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\) and every
3683	  \(p > 0\) one has \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). This is the seed that supplies
3684	  condition (3) of the basis-comparison Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}, and is the 01I8
3685	  globalisation of the tilde-case standard-cover vanishing.
3686	\end{lemma}
3687	\begin{proof}
3688	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
3689	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} the quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is isomorphic to
3690	  \(\widetilde{M}\) for \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\), with sections over each face \(D(g_\sigma)\)

3691	  the away localisation \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing of
3692	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} applies to the tilde sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\): its section
3693	  {\v C}ech complex over any standard cover is exact in positive degrees. Transporting along the
3694	  isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\), which is natural in the coefficient and so induces
3695	  an isomorphism of section {\v C}ech complexes, gives \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\)
3696	  for all \(p > 0\) and every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\). Hence \(\mathcal{F}\) has vanishing
3697	  higher {\v C}ech cohomology for the affine cover system.
3698	\end{proof}
3699	
3700	The {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis
3701	(Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}) is proved by a dimension shift on the
3702	coefficient module, decomposed below into four sub-lemmas: the short exact sequence of
3703	{\v C}ech complexes coming from a basis (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}), the
3704	resulting stability of vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology under the injective
3705	quotient (Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}), the sheaf-cohomology base case
3706	\(H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}), and the
3707	dimension-shift induction \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\)
3708	(Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}). The top lemma's proof then just
3709	chains these.
3710	
3711	\subsection{Section {\v C}ech infrastructure and the cover-system encoding}
3712	
3713	The sub-lemmas below are formalized in a \emph{cover-local}, presheaf-level, hypothesis-driven
3714	form: each lemma fixes a single index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) and works with the
3715	section {\v C}ech complexes of that one covering, taking the geometric inputs (section-level
3716	exactness on faces, positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing) as hypotheses. The cover-global
3717	\((\mathcal{B}, \mathrm{Cov})\) data of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis} re-enters only
3718	in the inductive step (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}), where it is packaged by
3719	the cover-system encoding (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system},
3720	Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}) and discharges those hypotheses via the per-face
3721	short-exact-sequence derivation (Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}). The supporting
3722	definitions and the degreewise product engine are collected first.
3723	
3724	\begin{definition}
3725	
3726	\leanok
3727	[{\v C}ech cohomology accessor]
3728	  \label{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
3729	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechCohomology}
3730	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
3731	  \textit{Project-local accessor.}
3732	  For an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) (giving the covering \(\mathcal{U}\)), a
3733	  presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(p \geq 0\), the \emph{{\v C}ech
3734	  cohomology} \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the degree-\(p\) homology of the section
3735	  {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex},
3736	  \[
3737	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \;:=\;
3738	      H^p\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr),
3739	  \]
3740	  taken as an object of \(\mathrm{Ab}\). This is the named wrapper that the 01EO chain refers to
3741	  uniformly.
3742	\end{definition}
3743	
3744	\begin{definition}
3745	[Functoriality of the section {\v C}ech complex]
3746	  \label{def:section_cech_functoriality}
3747	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCosimplicialMap,
3748	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor,
3749	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexFunctor,
3750	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexMap}
3751	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
3752	  \textit{Project-local.}
3753	  A morphism \(\varphi : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G}\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
3754	  acts on the section {\v C}ech data of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} coordinatewise:
3755	  on the basic-open section group of each intersection it is the map
3756	  \(\varphi(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) : \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) \to \mathcal{G}(U_{i_0 \ldots
3757	  i_p})\). These assemble into a cosimplicial morphism (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial
3758	  map}), functorial in \(\varphi\) (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial functor}). Composing
3759	  with the alternating-coface-map complex functor packages the section {\v C}ech complex as a
3760	  functor in the coefficient presheaf (the \emph{section {\v C}ech complex functor}) and yields,
3761	  for each \(\varphi\), the induced chain map of section {\v C}ech complexes
3762	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \varphi)\). These are the maps used to build the short
3763	  complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex}.
3764	\end{definition}
3765	
3766	\begin{definition}
3767	
3768	\leanok
3769	[Per-face section short complex]
3770	  \label{def:face_short_complex}
3771	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.faceShortComplex}
3772	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
3773	  \textit{Project-local building block.}
3774	  Let \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) be an index family and let
3775	  \(P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3\) be a short complex of presheaves of
3776	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. For a degree \(p\) and a tuple \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\)
3777	  write \(V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\) for the corresponding basic open. The \emph{per-face
3778	  short complex} \(\mathrm{face}(U, P, \sigma)\) is the short complex of abelian groups obtained by
3779	  evaluating \(P\) on sections over \(V_\sigma\),
3780	  \[
3781	    \mathcal{P}_1(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow \mathcal{P}_2(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow
3782	      \mathcal{P}_3(V_\sigma),
3783	  \]
3784	  the maps being the section-level actions over \(V_\sigma\) of the two maps of \(P\). It is the
3785	  \((p, \sigma)\)-indexed factor whose product over all tuples \(\sigma\) is the degree-\(p\) term
3786	  of the section {\v C}ech complex.
3787	\end{definition}
3788	
3789	\begin{definition}
3790	
3791	\leanok
3792	[Short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes]
3793	  \label{def:section_cech_short_complex}
3794	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexShortComplex}
3795	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:section_cech_functoriality}
3796	  \textit{Project-local.}
3797	  For an index family \(U\) and a short complex
3798	  \(P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3\) of presheaves of
3799	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the \emph{short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes} is
3800	  \[
3801	    0 \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_1) \to
3802	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_2) \to
3803	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_3) \to 0,
3804	  \]
3805	  a short complex in the category of cochain complexes of abelian groups, with the two maps the
3806	  chain maps induced by the maps of \(P\) through the functoriality of
3807	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_functoriality}. Its short-exactness is the conclusion of
3808	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis} and the input to Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}.
3809	\end{definition}

3811	\begin{definition}
3812	
3813	[Cover system on a basis]
3814	  \label{def:basis_cov_system}
3815	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.BasisCovSystem, AlgebraicGeometry.CovDatum}
3816	  \uses{def:cech_complex, lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:injective_cech_acyclic}
3817	  \textit{Project-bespoke encoding of the hypotheses of Stacks 01EO.}
3818	  A \emph{cover system on a basis} for a scheme \(X\) is a record with five fields. A
3819	  \emph{covering datum} (the helper \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\)) is a pair \((\iota, U)\) of an index type
3820	  \(\iota\) together with an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\); it is the raw shape
3821	  \(\Sigma\,\iota,\ \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) over which the section {\v C}ech complex is built.
3822	  The cover system bundles:
3823	  \begin{enumerate}
3824	  \item a basis \(\mathcal{B} \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) for the topology;
3825	  \item a set \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of admissible coverings, each a \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\);
3826	  \item \emph{faces-in-basis} (\(\mathrm{faces\_mem}\)): for every covering in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) every
3827	    finite intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) of its opens lies in \(\mathcal{B}\) (condition (1) of
3828	    Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis});
3829	  \item \emph{section surjectivity} (\(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\)): for every \(V \in \mathcal{B}\)
3830	    and every short exact sequence \(S : 0 \to S_1 \to S_2 \to S_3 \to 0\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
3831	    whose left term \(S_1\) has vanishing positive {\v C}ech cohomology over the coverings of \(V\) in
3832	    \(\mathrm{Cov}\), the section map \(S_2(V) \to S_3(V)\) is surjective;
3833	  \item \emph{injective acyclicity} (\(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\)): for every injective
3834	    \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) and every covering \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) the
3835	    positive-degree {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes, \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for
3836	    all \(p > 0\).
3837	  \end{enumerate}
3838	  Unlike a bare combinatorial record, the structure carries two sheaf-theoretic ({\v C}ech-cohomology)
3839	  fields. The field \(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\) is \emph{not} the raw cofinality datum: it is the
3840	  section-surjectivity \emph{output} that cofinality together with Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}
3841	  produces, packaged directly as the consequence the 01EO chain consumes. The field
3842	  \(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\) is the injective {\v C}ech-acyclicity of
3843	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} for the system's own coverings. These are exactly the data
3844	  that the dimension-shift induction Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} consumes.
3845	\end{definition}
3846	
3847	\begin{definition}
3848	
3849	\leanok
3850	[Vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for a cover system]
3851	  \label{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}
3852	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.HasVanishingHigherCech}
3853	  \uses{def:basis_cov_system, def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
3854	  \textit{Project-bespoke inductive predicate (condition (3) of Stacks 01EO, per module).}
3855	  Given a cover system \(s\) on a basis (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system}), an
3856	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for
3857	  \(s\)} when
3858	  \[
3859	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad
3860	      \text{for every } \mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov} \text{ and every } p > 0,
3861	  \]
3862	  with \(\check{H}^p\) the accessor of Definition~\ref{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}. This is the
3863	  per-module form of condition (3). It is deliberately stated for an \emph{arbitrary}
3864	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module: the dimension-shift induction of
3865	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} feeds the quotient \(\mathcal{Q} =
3866	  \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\) back as the coefficient, and \(\mathcal{Q}\) need not be quasi-coherent
3867	  even when \(\mathcal{F}\) is. The inductive class is therefore ``\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules with
3868	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)'', a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) entering only
3869	  as the seed at the affine (Tag 02KG) instantiation; the predicate must \emph{not} be specialized
3870	  to quasi-coherent modules.
3871	\end{definition}
3872	
3873	\begin{lemma}
3874	[Product of short exact sequences in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) (AB4*)]
3875	  \label{lem:short_exact_pi_map}
3876	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.shortExact_piMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pi_π_map_apply}
3877	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
3878	  \textit{Project AB4* lemma (Archon-original infrastructure).}
3879	  Let \((S_j)_{j \in J}\) be a family, indexed by an arbitrary type \(J\), of short exact sequences
3880	  of abelian groups, \(S_j : 0 \to A_j \xrightarrow{f_j} B_j \xrightarrow{g_j} C_j \to 0\). Then
3881	  the short complex assembled from the product maps,
3882	  \[
3883	    0 \to \prod_{j \in J} A_j \xrightarrow{\;\prod_j f_j\;} \prod_{j \in J} B_j
3884	      \xrightarrow{\;\prod_j g_j\;} \prod_{j \in J} C_j \to 0,
3885	  \]
3886	  is again short exact. This is the AB4* exactness-of-products property of \(\mathrm{Ab}\); it is
3887	  the degreewise engine of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}, since each {\v C}ech term is a product
3888	  of basic-open section groups (Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}).
3889	\end{lemma}
3890	\begin{proof}
3891	  Monomorphy of the product map \(\prod_j f_j\) is automatic, as products preserve monomorphisms.
3892	  Exactness at the middle is checked componentwise: an element of \(\prod_j B_j\) annihilated by
3893	  \(\prod_j g_j\) is annihilated in each coordinate \(j\), hence lies in the image of \(f_j\) by
3894	  exactness of \(S_j\), and the resulting coordinatewise preimages assemble to a preimage in
3895	  \(\prod_j A_j\). The one nontrivial point is epimorphy of \(\prod_j g_j\), which is \emph{not} an
3896	  automatic instance in \(\mathrm{Ab}\): given a target tuple in \(\prod_j C_j\), surjectivity of
3897	  each \(g_j\) supplies a coordinatewise preimage, and these assemble — through the canonical
3898	  identification of an \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued product with the set-theoretic product of its
3899	  components — to a preimage of the whole tuple. Hence \(\prod_j g_j\) is an epimorphism and the
3900	  displayed sequence is short exact.
3901	\end{proof}

3903	\begin{lemma}
3904	
3905	\leanok
3906	[Short exact sequence of {\v C}ech complexes from a basis]
3907	  \label{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}
3908	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis}
3909	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:face_short_complex, def:section_cech_short_complex,
3910	    lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:short_exact_pi_map}
3911	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
3912	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof, term-wise-product step) (read from
3913	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1695--1776).
3914	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \ref{lemma-ses-cech-h1} and our assumption (2)
3915	  % this sequence gives rise to an exact sequence
3916	  % $$
3917	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U) \to 0.
3918	  % $$
3919	  % for every $U \in \mathcal{B}$. Hence for any $\mathcal{U} \in \text{Cov}$

3921	  % $$
3922	  % 0 \to
3923	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \to
3924	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) \to
3925	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) \to 0

3930	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3931	  Fix an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) (giving the covering \(\mathcal{U}\)) and a
3932	  short complex
3933	  \[
3934	    P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3
3935	  \]
3936	  of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Assume that for every degree \(p\) and every tuple
3937	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) the per-face short complex of
3938	  Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex} — the section sequence
3939	  \[
3940	    \mathcal{P}_1(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow \mathcal{P}_2(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow
3941	      \mathcal{P}_3(V_\sigma), \qquad V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)
3942	  \]
3943	  — is short exact. Then the induced short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes of
3944	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex},
3945	  \[
3946	    0 \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_1) \to
3947	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_2) \to
3948	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_3) \to 0,
3949	  \]
3950	  is short exact (as a short complex of cochain complexes of abelian groups).
3951	
3952	  \emph{Remark.} In the cover-global \((\mathcal{B}, \mathrm{Cov})\) setting of
3953	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}, where \(P\) is the section short complex of a short
3954	  exact sequence \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) of sheaves, the per-face
3955	  hypothesis is discharged by the per-face short-exact-sequence derivation of
3956	  Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}: left-exactness of the section sequence over \(V_\sigma\) is
3957	  automatic, and surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\) is the output of
3958	  Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} at the basic open \(V_\sigma\) (condition (1) placing \(V_\sigma \in
3959	  \mathcal{B}\)).

3962	\begin{proof}
3963	  \uses{def:section_cech_short_complex, def:face_short_complex, lem:short_exact_pi_map}
3964	  \leanok
3965	  Short-exactness of a short complex of cochain complexes is checked degreewise: it suffices to
3966	  verify short-exactness in each cohomological degree \(p\). In degree \(p\) the term of the
3967	  section {\v C}ech complex is the product \(\prod_{\sigma}(-)(V_\sigma)\) over all tuples
3968	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of section groups over the basic opens \(V_\sigma\), and
3969	  the two maps are the products of the corresponding per-face maps; thus the degree-\(p\) short
3970	  complex is the product over \(\sigma\) of the per-face short complexes
3971	  (Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex}). Each of these is short exact by hypothesis, so their
3972	  product is short exact by the AB4* product-of-short-exact-sequences lemma
3973	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:short_exact_pi_map}). As this holds in every degree, the short complex of section
3974	  {\v C}ech complexes (Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex}) is short exact.
3975	\end{proof}

3977	\begin{lemma}
3978	
3979	[Per-face short exact sequence from a sheaf short exact sequence]
3980	  \label{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}
3981	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses,
3982	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor}
3983	  \uses{lem:ses_cech_h1, def:face_short_complex}
3984	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
3985	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof, section-exactness step) (read from
3986	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1729--1734).
3987	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \ref{lemma-ses-cech-h1} and our assumption (2)
3988	  % this sequence gives rise to an exact sequence
3989	  % $$
3990	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U) \to 0.
3991	  % $$
3992	  % for every $U \in \mathcal{B}$."
3993	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO,
3994	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3995	  Let \(S : 0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of
3996	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, let \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) be an index family,
3997	  and suppose that on every face \(V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\) the section map
3998	  \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\) is surjective — the basis-surjectivity datum
3999	  supplied by Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}. Writing \(P\) for the section short complex of \(S\)
4000	  regarded as a short complex of presheaves of modules, the per-face short complex of
4001	  Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex} is then short exact for every degree \(p\) and tuple
4002	  \(\sigma\). This lemma produces the per-face hypothesis consumed by
4003	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}, and is the bridge by which
4004	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} instantiates the cover-local lemmas from a
4005	  genuine sheaf short exact sequence.
4006	\end{lemma}
4007	\begin{proof}
4008	  \uses{lem:ses_cech_h1, def:face_short_complex}
4009	  Fix \(p\) and \(\sigma\), and put \(V = V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\). Monomorphy of
4010	  \(\mathcal{F}(V) \to \mathcal{I}(V)\) and exactness at the middle of \(\mathcal{F}(V) \to
4011	  \mathcal{I}(V) \to \mathcal{Q}(V)\) are the left-exactness of the sections functor applied to the
4012	  short exact sequence of sheaves \(S\): the sections functor \(\mathcal{G} \mapsto \mathcal{G}(V)\)
4013	  is the composite of the inclusion of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of

4281	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{B}\) be a basis for its topology, let

4285	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \in \mathcal{B}\); (2) for each
4286	  \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(U\) occurring in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are
4287	  cofinal among all open coverings of \(U\); and (3) for every \(\mathcal{U} \in
4288	  \mathrm{Cov}\) the {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes,
4289	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). Then
4290	  \[

4292	      U \in \mathcal{B}.

4397	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
4398	
4399	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
4400	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
4401	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
4402	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
4403	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
4404	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.

4406	\begin{lemma}
4407	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
4408	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
4409	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
4410	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
4411	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma

4413	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
4414	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
4415	  % \begin{equation}
4416	  % \label{equation-extended}
4417	  % 0 \to
4418	  % M \to
4419	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
4420	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
4421	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
4422	  % \ldots
4423	  % \end{equation}
4424	  % (whose truncation we have studied in

4426	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
4427	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see

4429	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
4430	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
4431	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
4432	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
4433	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
4434	  \[
4435	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
4436	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
4437	    \qquad
4438	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
4439	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
4440	  \]
4441	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
4442	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
4443	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
4444	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
4445	\end{lemma}
4446	
4447	\begin{proof}
4448	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
4449	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
4450	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
4451	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
4452	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
4453	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
4454	  localisations
4455	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
4456	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
4457	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
4458	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
4459	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
4460	\end{proof}

4462	\begin{lemma}
4463	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
4464	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
4465	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
4466	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
4467	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
4468	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
4469	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
4470	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
4471	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
4472	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
4473	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
4474	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
4475	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
4476	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
4477	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
4478	  \[
4479	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
4480	      \;\cong\;
4481	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
4482	  \]
4483	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
4484	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
4485	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
4486	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
4487	\end{lemma}
4488	
4489	\begin{proof}
4490	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
4491	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
4492	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
4493	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
4494	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
4495	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
4496	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
4497	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
4498	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
4499	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
4500	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
4501	  additive homology-preserving functor.
4502	\end{proof}

4504	\begin{lemma}
4505	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
4506	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
4507	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology,
4508	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex}
4509	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
4510	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
4511	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
4512	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
4513	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
4514	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
4515	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
4516	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
4517	  % associated to the presheaves
4518	  % $$
4519	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
4520	  % $$
4521	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
4522	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
4523	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
4524	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
4525	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
4526	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
4527	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
4528	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
4529	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
4530	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
4531	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
4532	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
4533	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
4534	  \[
4535	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
4536	      \;\cong\;
4537	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
4538	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
4539	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
4540	  \]
4541	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
4542	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
4543	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
4544	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
4545	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
4546	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
4547	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
4548	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
4549	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
4550	
4551	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
4552	  of the preimage,
4553	  \[
4554	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
4555	      \;=\;
4556	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
4557	  \]
4558	  which uses that \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is again injective over
4559	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the
4560	  resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form.
4561	\end{lemma}
4562	
4563	\begin{proof}
4564	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
4565	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
4566	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
4567	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
4568	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
4569	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
4570	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
4571	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
4572	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
4573	  \[
4574	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
4575	      \;\cong\;
4576	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
4577	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
4578	  \]
4579	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
4580	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
4581	  affine-local vanishing criterion.
4582	\end{proof}

4590	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
4591	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,

4593	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.

4597	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
4598	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
4599	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
4600	  \to S\). Then:
4601	  \begin{enumerate}
4602	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
4603	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
4604	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
4605	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
4606	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
4607	      \(k \geq 0\),
4608	      \[
4609	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
4610	      \]
4611	  \end{enumerate}
4612	\end{lemma}
4613	
4614	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
4615	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
4616	% the sheaf
4617	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
4618	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
4619	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
4620	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
4621	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
4622	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
4623	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
4624	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
4625	\begin{proof}
4626	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
4627	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
4628	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
4629	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
4630	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
4631	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
4632	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
4633	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
4634	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
4635	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
4636	
4637	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
4638	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
4639	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
4640	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
4641	
4642	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
4643	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
4644	  sheafification of
4645	  \[
4646	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
4647	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
4648	  \]
4649	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
4650	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
4651	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
4652	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
4653	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
4654	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
4655	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
4656	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
4657	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
4658	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
4659	
4660	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
4661	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
4662	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
4663	  for every \(k\),
4664	  \[
4665	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
4666	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
4667	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
4668	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
4669	  \]
4670	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
4671	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
4672	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
4673	\end{proof}

{
  "id": "def:qcoh_sections_localized",
  "type": "definition",
  "title": "Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation",
  "chapter": "{\v C",
  "statement": "\textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
  items (4)--(5).}
  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).

  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.

  Two combinatorial--geometric ingredients package the identification into the form
  consumed by Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}. First, the multi-index
  intersection identity
  \(\bigl(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\bigr) = D\bigl(\prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\bigr)\) in
  \((\operatorname{Spec} R).\mathrm{Opens}\) (\(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\), proved
  by induction on the index length using the infimum-splitting helper
  \(\operatorname{iInf\_fin\_succ}\) and \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen\_mul}\)):
  it rewrites the \((p+1)\)-fold intersection over a multi-index \(\sigma\) as the single
  basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\), so the
  section group there is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). Second, the restriction
  compatibility of item~(5) is made explicit: for a basic-open inclusion
  \(D(b) \subseteq D(a)\) the presheaf restriction map of \(\widetilde{M}\) between the
  two section groups \emph{is} the away-localisation comparison
  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\) of the two localisation units
  (\(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\), by the uniqueness
  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) of a map of localised modules);
  this is the single differential brick that the {\v C}ech coface match of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} feeds on.

  The identification is packaged by a few helpers: the two localised-module endpoints
  \(\operatorname{tP}, \operatorname{tU}\) (the section groups
  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) named as away localisations at the two indices
  involved in a face), the per-\(\sigma\) comparison
  \(\varphi_\sigma = \operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\) realised both as the bare
  localisation comparison \(\operatorname{phiL}\) and as its sheaf-level avatar
  \(\operatorname{phi}\) (their agreement is \(\operatorname{phi\_eq\_phiL}\)), and the
  accessor-bridge \(\operatorname{restr\_bridge}\) that exhibits the presheaf
  restriction map through these comparisons.",
  "uses": [
    "def:section_cech_complex",
    "def:standard_affine_cover"
  ],
  "lean_name": "AlgebraicGeometry.qcohSectionsAwayLocalized, AlgebraicGeometry.basicOpen_sprod, AlgebraicGeometry.iInf_fin_succ, AlgebraicGeometry.qcohRestriction_eq_comparison, AlgebraicGeometry.tP, AlgebraicGeometry.tU, AlgebraicGeometry.phiL, AlgebraicGeometry.phi, AlgebraicGeometry.phi_eq_phiL, AlgebraicGeometry.restr_bridge",
  "proved": false,
  "mathlib_ok": false,
  "proof_tex": "",
  "lean_source": "lemma qcohSectionsAwayLocalized {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u}
    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {n : ℕ} (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom := by
  rw [basicOpen_sprod]
  infer_instance

lemma basicOpen_sprod {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (n : ℕ) (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (∏ k, s (σ k)) := by
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      rw [show (∏ k, s (σ k)) = 1 from Finset.prod_of_isEmpty _]
      simp only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]
      exact iInf_of_empty _
  | succ m ih =>
      rw [Fin.prod_univ_succ, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, ← ih (fun i => σ i.succ),
        iInf_fin_succ]
      rfl

private lemma iInf_fin_succ {α : Type*} [CompleteLattice α] (n : ℕ) (f : Fin (n + 1) → α) :
    (⨅ i, f i) = f 0 ⊓ ⨅ i : Fin n, f i.succ := by
  apply le_antisymm
  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun i => iInf_le _ i.succ)
  · refine le_iInf fun i => ?_
    refine Fin.cases inf_le_left (fun j => le_trans inf_le_right (iInf_le _ j)) i

lemma qcohRestriction_eq_comparison {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {a b : R}
    (i : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b : (Spec R).Opens) ⟶ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
    (hb : AwayComparison.Inverts a
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)))) :
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom := inferInstance
  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique
    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb _ ?_).symm
  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h

private noncomputable abbrev tP : (Spec R).PresheafOfModules :=
  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M)

private abbrev tU : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R) :=
  fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)

private noncomputable def phiL {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃ₗ[R] dCoeff s M σ :=
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
  (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm

private noncomputable def phi {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃+ dCoeff s M σ :=
  (phiL M s σ).toAddEquiv

private lemma phi_eq_phiL {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι)
    (y : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))) :
    phi M s σ y = phiL M s σ y := rfl

private lemma restr_bridge (V W : (Spec R).Opens) (g : V ⟶ W)
    (x : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom ((tP M).presheaf.map g.op) x
      = (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map g.op).hom) x := rfl

  "statement": "ok
[Quasi-coherent sheaves on an affine are sections-tilde]
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Tag 01HV, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves}.}
  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Writing \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global sections,
  there is a natural isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
  under which \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F}) = M_f\) for every \(f \in R\); in particular the sections of
  \(\mathcal{F}\) over a distinguished open \(D(f)\) are the away localisation \(M_f\). This is the
  affine quasi-coherent structure theorem (Tag 01HV, with the equivalence quasi-coherent-on-affine
  \(\simeq\) \(R\)-modules, Tag 01I8).",
  "uses": [
    "def:standard_affine_cover",
    "lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation"
  ],
  "lean_name": "AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_iso_tilde_sections, AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_hom, AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_inv",
  "proved": true,
  "mathlib_ok": false,
  "proof_tex": "
  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}
  \leanok
  On an affine scheme the global-sections functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) and
  the tilde functor \(M \mapsto \widetilde{M}\) are mutually inverse equivalences between
  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and \(R\)-modules; the counit
  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\) is an isomorphism for quasi-coherent
  \(\mathcal{F}\). By Tag 01HV the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(f)\) are \(M_f\), so the
  isomorphism identifies \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) with \(M_f\).

  Concretely, the isomorphism is the inverse of the tilde–\(\Gamma\) counit: once the counit
  \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma} : \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\) is known to
  be an isomorphism, the asserted iso \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})}\) is its
  inverse (the one-line conditional argument formalized). The hypothesis that the counit is an
  isomorphism holds for any \(\mathcal{F}\) admitting a global presentation
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}), and unconditionally for quasi-coherent
  \(\mathcal{F}\) once the 01I8 instance \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow
  \operatorname{IsIso}(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma})\) is supplied (see the decomposition remark
  below).
",

  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm

@[simp]
lemma qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_inv (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
    (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).inv = F.fromTildeΓ :=
  rfl",

### `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` — HARD GATE

`{ι}[Finite ι](U : ι → Opens X)` with **no covering hypothesis***. The two-part proof sketch is
present and internally coherent:

  (`lem:injective_of_adjoint` + `lem:mod_pmod_adjunction`).
